title: Daniele Cannatella
text:
Hi! I’m Daniele Cannatella, an Assistant Professor in Urban Data for Design at TU Delft, in the Department of Urbanism. My work sits at the intersection of cities, data, and design — I’m especially interested in how computational tools can help us better understand and shape urban environments.
I’m passionate about combining design thinking with data-driven approaches to support more inclusive, creative, and informed urban futures. I enjoy working across disciplines, experimenting with digital tools, and translating complex spatial information into meaningful insights for planning and design. Learn more
I also run Rbanism, a platform where I share research, tools, and reflections on data-driven urbanism.

title: Courses I Teach
text:
contribute to several courses across the Bachelor and Master curricula at TU Delft, particularly within the programs of Urbanism, Landscape Architecture, and Geomatics.
My teaching focuses on GIS, urban data science, spatial analysis, and spatial planning and design. I integrate mapping, digital cartography, data-driven approaches, research-by-design, and climate adaptation strategies into both undergraduate and graduate education.

title: Courses I Teach
text:
contribute to several courses across the Bachelor and Master curricula at TU Delft, particularly within the programs of Urbanism, Landscape Architecture, and Geomatics.
My teaching focuses on GIS, urban data science, spatial analysis, and spatial planning and design. I integrate mapping, digital cartography, data-driven approaches, research-by-design, and climate adaptation strategies into both undergraduate and graduate education.

title: Courses I Teach
text:
Bachelor Courses

IOP 1 - GeoExpert Role (Year 3)

Role: Course Instructor

Description: Introduction to data analysis and visualization for urban applications using GIS. Focus on spatial thinking, map design, and interpreting patterns in urban environments.
Tools: QGIS
Assessment: Practical assignments and group project

Period: Q1

title: Courses I Teach
text:
Master Courses

GIS and Urban Design

Role: Lecturer

Description: Advanced applications of GIS in design contexts, focusing on urban morphology, spatial analysis, and participatory tools.
Tools: QGIS, ArcGIS Pro, R
Target Group: MSc Urbanism, Landscape Architecture

Period: Q2

title: Courses I Teach
section: ### Data-Informed Design for Sustainable Urban Futures Graduation Cluster – Urbanism Track
text:
### Data-Informed Design for Sustainable Urban Futures Graduation Cluster – Urbanism Track

Role: Cluster Coordinator

Themes: Climate adaptation, nature-based solutions, peri-urban landscapes

Approach: Research-by-design, mixed methods, cross-scalar analysis

title: Courses I Teach
text:
Supervision & Studio Mentoring
I regularly supervise BSc and MSc theses related to: - Landscape-based strategies for climate resilience - Data-supported urban design - GIS-based analysis of socio-ecological systems

For more details on student work, see Supervised Work

title: Courses I Teach
text:
Want to Collaborate?
I’m always happy to explore guest lectures or co-teaching opportunities in topics such as: - Urban data science - Spatial analytics - Strategic design for climate adaptation
Feel free to get in touch if you’re interested in collaboration.
